items.prompt: Solve `x + 7 = 19` and show how each step preserves equality.
items.expected_evidence: identify which quantity is treated as the input-like or driving quantity in a bounded relationship
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates identify which quantity is treated as the input-like or driving quantity in a bounded relationship with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Solve `4x = 28`, then check the solution by substitution.
items.expected_evidence: identify which quantity changes in response and how it is recorded
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates identify which quantity changes in response and how it is recorded with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Match the equation `n - 5 = 12` to a balance or missing-value situation and solve it.
items.expected_evidence: explain paired values as related measures of one relationship rather than as separate facts
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates explain paired values as related measures of one relationship rather than as separate facts with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain why subtracting the same value from both sides of an equation preserves the solution set.
items.expected_evidence: identify which quantity is treated as the input-like or driving quantity in a bounded relationship
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to identify which quantity is treated as the input-like or driving quantity in a bounded relationship.

items.prompt: Explain what the variable represents and why inverse operations help isolate it.
items.expected_evidence: identify which quantity changes in response and how it is recorded
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to identify which quantity changes in response and how it is recorded.

items.prompt: A student solves `x + 5 = 13` by writing `x = 13 + 5`. Diagnose the first structural mistake. Focus on variable role swap.
items.expected_evidence: identify which quantity is treated as the input-like or driving quantity in a bounded relationship
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward identify which quantity is treated as the input-like or driving quantity in a bounded relationship.

items.prompt: A student solves `3x = 18` by dividing only the `x`. Explain what went wrong. Focus on paired value fragmentation.
items.expected_evidence: identify which quantity changes in response and how it is recorded
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward identify which quantity changes in response and how it is recorded.

items.prompt: A gym charges a $12 signup fee plus $5 per class. Write and solve an equation for the number of classes if the total was $32.
items.expected_evidence: identify which quantity is treated as the input-like or driving quantity in a bounded relationship
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves identify which quantity is treated as the input-like or driving quantity in a bounded relationship in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: A balance model has three equal bags and 6 loose counters on one side and 24 counters on the other. Write and solve the equation.
items.expected_evidence: identify which quantity changes in response and how it is recorded
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves identify which quantity changes in response and how it is recorded in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: Two weeks later, solve `m/3 = 8` and explain how you know the result is reasonable.
items.expected_evidence: identify which quantity is treated as the input-like or driving quantity in a bounded relationship
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates identify which quantity is treated as the input-like or driving quantity in a bounded relationship on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Before solving variable relationships and independent dependent quantities, complete a prerequisite check on variable expressions and substitution and explain how it supports the current equation work.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether variable expressions and substitution is secure enough to support variable relationships and independent dependent quantities
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in variable expressions and substitution from a current-node issue in variable relationships and independent dependent quantities.

items.prompt: Use a quick linear relationship tables and graphs check to explain whether the learner is blocked by prerequisite structure or by variable relationships and independent dependent quantities itself.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether linear relationship tables and graphs is secure enough to support variable relationships and independent dependent quantities
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in linear relationship tables and graphs from a current-node issue in variable relationships and independent dependent quantities.
